findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:151 Gavin calls the battle 'small'; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:189 the narration then explains at length what that means about him.
findings.problem: The chapter's most original moral beat lands in the scene, then gets restated in thesis language. After Gavin already says the indefensible thing, the narration spells out that he is 'something else' and paraphrases the chapter's irony for the reader. That lowers the discomfort instead of trusting it, which keeps the confrontation below shortlist-level force.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the confrontation beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:141 and landing by snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:190 so Gavin states his hierarchy once, Miren answers from her own immediate stakes, and the narration stops translating the scene into abstract verdicts after her silence. Keep his self-correction and flatness, but cut or compress the explanatory summary at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:189 so her look, her injuries, and her exit carry the horror.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:141 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:190, Gavin's refusal is legible from his spoken lines and Miren's reaction without a narrator sentence that labels him as 'something else' or restates that he could end the war and will not; fail if the revised passage still explains the moral meaning after the scene has already shown it.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:239 Gavin finds the place he has been reading wrong; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:247 dawn arrives; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:249 the battle is already in aftermath mode; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:263 he is back in the sub-collection chasing the pattern.
findings.problem: The chapter overruns its boundary and contradicts the supplied handoff into chapter 18. The boundary pack says chapter 18 opens at dawn with the substrate finally quiet and Gavin realizing he accomplished nothing during the battle; this chapter instead gives him a dawn breakthrough, aftermath tableau, and return to the sub-collection before the handoff. That is a canonical continuity break, not just overlap.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework or relocate the late section beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:217 and ending by snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:263 so chapter 17 hands off before the chapter-18 material begins. Options: end this chapter once Voldemort falls and Gavin recognizes the noise is easing, or let him reach the sub-collection still chasing the pattern unsuccessfully. Do not keep the dawn breakthrough at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:239, the dawn aftermath soundscape at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:247 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:249, and the successful note-taking before handoff if chapter 18 is still meant to begin with 'By dawn the substrate had gone quiet' and 'He'd accomplished nothing.'
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, by the end of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md, Gavin has not yet reached the chapter-18 dawn state: he has not already named the key misreading, written breakthrough notes at dawn, or moved through the full post-battle aftermath that the boundary excerpt assigns to chapter 18. Fail if those beats remain on-page in chapter 17.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:43 the Ravenclaw dies or is incapacitated offstage as 'the corridor was quiet'; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:197 a wounded person is reduced to a 'wet choking sound'; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:249 aftermath grief arrives mostly as distant noises.
findings.problem: The battle's human cost is repeatedly softened into offscreen sound cues and euphemistic endpoints right where the novel most needs concrete consequence. Because this book's engine depends on holding Gavin's ward obsession against fully rendered suffering, the chapter cannot let decisive damage dissolve into 'the corridor was quiet' and distant choking. Here the omission reads as sanitization drift rather than intensifying restraint.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the battle-contact beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:39 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:43, plus the corridor aftermath at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:197 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:201, to keep Gavin emotionally and morally distant while rendering at least one immediate battle consequence with concrete bodily detail in his direct sensorium. He can still look away, keep walking, or resume work; the fix is not to make him heroic but to stop euphemizing the cost he is ignoring.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised chapter includes at least one on-page injury, body, or active triage consequence between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:39 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:43 or between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:197 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:201 that a reader can visualize without inference, and Gavin still chooses the wards over that reality. Fail if those beats remain legible only as vague quiet, distant noise, or summary.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:7, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:13, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:15, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:45, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:55, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:209, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:219, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:237, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:257 all reuse the same comparison lead-in built around 'the way...'.
findings.problem: The chapter leans on the same analogy scaffold far past the allowed threshold. Extraordinary perception keeps getting filtered through identical 'the way...' constructions, which turns a useful explanatory move into a prose tic and flattens the chapter's rhythm. The repeated sentence mold is especially visible because the chapter is otherwise trying to sell novelty of perception.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the descriptive passages beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:7, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:45, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:209 so the substrate is not repeatedly introduced through the same 'the way...' comparison template. Keep a few analogies where they do real work, but let other moments land as direct perception, different syntax, or sharper physical observation by the time those sections resolve at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:15, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:57, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:257.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a reader can move through the revised spans between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:7 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:15, between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:45 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:57, and between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:209 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:257 without encountering the same 'the way...' construction three or more times, and at least half of those descriptions arrive without that template. Fail if the chapter still repeatedly returns to the same analogy lead-in as its default sentence shape.

summary: Dialogue passes: Gavin and Miren stay distinct, contracted, and pressure-true, with Gavin's hedging/self-correction and Miren's directness both matching the style bible. The chapter still fails overall because it softens too much battle consequence into euphemistic distance, over-explains its own central moral shock, repeats a visible prose formula, and breaks the supplied chapter-18 boundary by staging the dawn breakthrough too early.
